reconciliation.round1_summary: Class TN: §2.2 FirstOrderKMS.gamma_nonnegativity does not exist (FirstOrderKMS is a structure with fields fdr_i1, fdr_i2, i3_zero, etc. — no field 'gamma_nonnegativity')

reconciliation.round1_summary: Class IA: Heidelberg delta_diss = 10^-3 in §3.5 contradicts hierarchy table value ~26% — four orders of magnitude. Spectral-floor claim 'omega_x ~ 6 T_H' depends on this.
reconciliation.round2_evidence: Paper §3.5 (lines 416-418) now reads 'For Heidelberg-class BEC (delta_diss ≈ 0.26; Tab. hierarchy), omega_x ≈ T_H ln(2/0.26) ≈ 2.0 T_H'. Numerically: ln(2/0.26) = ln(7.692) = 2.040 — matches '~2.0 T_H'. Internally consistent with Table 1 line 458 which lists Heidelberg delta_diss ~ 26%. The previous '~6 T_H' claim was eliminated.

deterministic_recheck.result: Match in abstract (line 59) and Table 1 (line 460); both -19%.

sentences.quote: The KMS structure is encoded as the \texttt{FirstOrderKMS} record in \texttt{SecondOrderSK.lean}
sentences.quote_normalized: the kms structure is encoded as the firstorderkms record in secondordersk.lean
sentences.agent_notes: TN_R2_NEW (advisory): Round-1 fix introduced a module-attribution error. The text says FirstOrderKMS is encoded in SecondOrderSK.lean, but FirstOrderKMS is actually defined in SKDoubling.lean (line 367). The field references (fdr_i1, fdr_i2, i3_zero) ARE correct (those fields are in SKDoubling.FirstOrderKMS). Recommend: change 'in SecondOrderSK.lean' to 'in SKDoubling.lean' for accuracy, or replace with the qualified-name form 'as the SKDoubling.FirstOrderKMS record'. Non-blocking because the fields exist and resolve correctly under the canonical SKDoubling module; this is an attribution polish.

sentences.quote: graphene delivers a closed-form noise spectrum $\Delta S_I(\omega)$ ... derived independently from Keldysh fluctuation-dissipation and Landauer-Büttiker scattering
sentences.quote_normalized: graphene delivers a closed-form noise spectrum derived independently from keldysh fluctuation-dissipation and landauer-buttiker scattering
sentences.agent_notes: TN_R2_NEW (advisory): Round-1 body fix correctly downgraded the §6.5 prose to cite only GrapheneNoiseFormula.fdt_consistency (the existing Lean theorem). However, the abstract retains the 'two routes' claim ('derived independently from Keldysh FDT and Landauer-Büttiker scattering'). The L-B route has no corresponding Lean theorem. Two acceptable resolutions: (a) soften the abstract to 'derived from Keldysh fluctuation-dissipation, with a Landauer-Büttiker companion derivation in prose only'; or (b) add the L-B route theorem in a follow-up Lean wave and a routes_agree bridge. Non-blocking because the abstract claim is technically a methodology statement (two prose derivations exist; only one is formalized). Same root issue as Round-1 block:3.

sentences.quote: The single substantive axiom in the chain (\texttt{FirstOrderKMS}) was reformulated after Aristotle's counterexample $c = (0,0,0,0,0,0,0,1,0)$
sentences.quote_normalized: the single substantive axiom in the chain (firstorderkms) was reformulated after aristotle's counterexample c = (0,0,0,0,0,0,0,1,0)
sentences.agent_notes: TN advisory carried over from Round-1 advisory:4. counts.json still lists 1 Lean axiom (gapped_interface_axiom in D2/D4 territory) — NOT FirstOrderKMS. FirstOrderKMS is a Prop structure, not an `axiom` declaration. This was advisory-level in Round 1 and was not addressed by Round-1 commit. Recommend a small wording change (e.g., 'the load-bearing tracked Prop in the chain (FirstOrderKMS) was reformulated...'). Non-blocking; structural-Prop-vs-axiom is a classification subtlety.

sentences.section: Exact WKB connection: three non-perturbative effects
sentences.agent_notes: Citation advisory carried over from Round-1 advisory:1. CorleyJacobson1996, CoutantParentani2012, RobertsonParentani2015, BelgiornoCacciatori2024 are still NOT in CITATION_REGISTRY. Bibitems exist locally in D1 (lines 855-875), so latex compile is fine. validate.py CHECK 1 (citation_integrity) will fail unless the 4 keys are added to CITATION_REGISTRY OR the paper is re-aliased to the canonical Corley1996 / CoutantParentani2010 keys. This is a known open advisory; not blocking submission but blocking the validate.py gate.

sentences.quote: The 1+1D BEC infrastructure of \S\ref{sec:bec-first}--\S\ref{sec:exact-wkb} contains $119$ theorems. Of these, $109$ apply to the graphene Dirac-fluid 2+1D setup unchanged ... yields a precise $92\%$ reuse fraction.

summary.agent_text: Round-2 reconciliation review: ALL 8 Round-1 BLOCKERs auto-closed via deterministic rechecks (lean_deps_lookup, registry_lookup, arithmetic_recompute, grep_paper). 0 open BLOCKERs. 5 advisory findings: (1) NEW: FirstOrderKMS module attributed to SecondOrderSK.lean but actually in SKDoubling.lean (advisory polish); (2) NEW: abstract retains 'two routes' graphene noise claim while body backs only one Lean route (consistent with Round-1 fix recommendation); (3) carry-over: FirstOrderKMS labeled 'single substantive axiom' but is a Prop structure; (4) carry-over: 4 WKB bibkeys (CorleyJacobson1996 etc.) not in CITATION_REGISTRY; (5) carry-over: 'precise 92%' wording on 109/119=91.6% reuse claim. Verdict: GREEN.
